content:
# GPU Inference Saturation Plan — Reference

Plan file: `docs/plans/2026-04-03-inference-gpu-saturation.md`
Branch: not yet created — plan only, implementation pending

## 5 tasks

1. **`RauneNetInference.infer_batch` + `infer_batch_gpu`** — `raune_net.py`; building block; `infer_batch_gpu` returns `(Tensor(N,3,H,W), w, h)` on device, no dtoh
2. **`DepthAnythingInference.infer_batch_from_tensors`** — `depth_anything.py`; accepts GPU tensor from RAUNE, GPU-resize+renorm on device, returns depth maps; also adds pinned memory to existing `infer_batch`
3. **`raune_depth_batch` protocol + server handler** — `protocol.py` adds `RauneDepthBatchResult`; `server.py` adds handler calling `infer_batch_gpu` then `infer_batch_from_tensors`
4. **`RauneDepthBatchItem` + `run_raune_depth_batch`** — `inference_subprocess.rs`; mirrors depth_batch pattern
5. **`auto_calibrate` refactor** — `grade.rs`; three phases: collect pixels → fused batch (FUSED_BATCH_SIZE=8) → push to store; IPC drops from 110 to 7 round-trips

## Test approach
pytest in `repos/dorea/python/tests/test_infer_batch.py` — CPU mode, mocked models, no GPU or weights required. Rust: compile-only struct tests in `#[cfg(test)]` block.
